18.reason: [The initial value of 'top' is 0, so an empty stack should be 'top == 0', not 'top == -1'. This causes incorrect detection of empty stack.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The initial value of 'top' is 0, so an empty stack should be 'top == 0', not 'top == -1'. This causes incorrect detection of empty stack.]

46.reason: [After pushing, 'top' points to the next empty slot, so the top element is at 'stack[top - 1]', not 'stack[top]'. This accesses an uninitialized or out-of-bound element.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After pushing, 'top' points to the next empty slot, so the top element is at 'stack[top - 1]', not 'stack[top]'. This accesses an uninitialized or out-of-bound element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After pushing, 'top' points to the next available slot, so the top element is at index 'top - 1', not 'top'. This accesses an uninitialized or out-of-bound element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After pushing, 'top' points to the next empty slot, so the top element is at index 'top-1', not 'top'. This accesses out-of-bounds memory.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After pushing, 'top' points to the next empty slot. So the top element is at 'top-1', not 'top'. This accesses an uninitialized or out-of-bound element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When popping, the top element is at index 'top - 1', not 'top', because 'top' points to the next available slot. Accessing stack[top] reads an uninitialized or already popped value.]
